activities_projects.title: Functions of Political Parties Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Analyse how political parties perform their functions in democracy
steps: List the major functions of political parties
Explain each function with an example
Discuss which function is most important and why
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students analyse the role of parties in democratic governance
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of explanation and reasoning (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Party System Comparison Table
activities_projects.objective: Compare different party systems in the world
steps: Create a table comparing one-party, two-party and multiparty systems
Write features of each system
Give examples of countries following each system
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand different political party systems

activities_projects.title: National Party Profile
activities_projects.objective: Study the ideology and role of a national political party
steps: Select one national party mentioned in the chapter
Write about its founding year, leader and ideology
Explain its role in Indian politics
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students gain deeper knowledge of political parties in India

activities_projects.title: State vs National Parties
activities_projects.objective: Understand the difference between national and state parties
steps: Write the criteria for national party recognition
Write the criteria for state party recognition
List two examples of each and compare their influence
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the classification of political parties

activities_projects.title: Challenges to Political Parties Mind Map
activities_projects.objective: Identify and organise the major challenges faced by political parties
steps: Write the four major challenges mentioned in the chapter
Create a mind map connecting these challenges
Add examples to explain each challenge
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand problems in political party functioning

activities_projects.title: Election Campaign Observation
activities_projects.objective: Understand how political parties communicate with voters
steps: Observe posters, speeches or news about an election campaign
Identify which political party is involved
Write three strategies used to influence voters
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn practical aspects of election campaigning

activities_projects.title: Debate on Multiparty System
activities_projects.objective: Evaluate advantages and disadvantages of multiparty systems
steps: Write arguments supporting multiparty systems
Write arguments against multiparty systems
Participate in a class debate
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop analytical thinking about political systems

activities_projects.title: Political Party Symbol Study
activities_projects.objective: Understand the significance of party symbols in elections
steps: List five election symbols used by political parties
Identify the party associated with each symbol
Explain why symbols are important for voters
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand how symbols help voters identify parties

activities_projects.title: Reforms for Political Parties
activities_projects.objective: Analyse possible reforms for improving political parties
steps: List the reforms suggested in the chapter
Explain how each reform can improve democracy
Suggest one additional reform of your own
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop critical thinking about political reforms

activities_projects.title: Political Party Survey
activities_projects.objective: Understand public opinion about political parties
steps: Ask five people which political party they support
Record their reasons
Analyse the common factors influencing their choice
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn how public opinion influences politics
